artifacts.description: Typed, versioned definition of a multi-step automation. Compiles to a stable execution graph the runtime can replay from events.
artifacts.trust_boundary: plugin-authored static definition; loaded read-only at runtime; never model-authored during a run

artifacts.description: Atomic unit of execution inside a Phase. Three variants (synthesis, checkpoint, dispatch) discriminated by kind.
artifacts.trust_boundary: plugin-authored static definition; loaded read-only at runtime

artifacts.description: Named, ordered grouping of Steps within a Workflow. May align with canonical spine or be workflow-specific.
artifacts.trust_boundary: plugin-authored static definition; loaded read-only at runtime

artifacts.description: Append-only sequence of events for a single Run. First event is bootstrap; sequence is 0-based contiguous; closure is singleton with no post-closure events.
writers: circuit-next engine (event append during run execution)
artifacts.trust_boundary: operator-local persisted state; append-only; never truncated in normal operation

artifacts.description: In-memory {log, snapshot} aggregate used for internal consistency checks. Not persisted. The log persists at run.log (events.ndjson); the snapshot persists at run.snapshot (state.json). RunProjection exists only to state the invariants that bind the two in memory (events_consumed equals log length; bootstrap-frozen fields match; closure-to-status mapping is total by construction).
artifacts.trust_boundary: engine-computed in-memory aggregate; always recomputable from run.log; never authoritative over the log; never persisted to disk (Slice 26a binding split — run.snapshot owns state.json)

artifacts.description: Reducer-derived run-local state checkpoint persisted at <circuit-next-run-root>/state.json. Always recomputable from run.log by replaying events; never independently authored. Does NOT include the event log itself — for the in-memory {log, snapshot} aggregate see run.projection. Slice 27c lands the reducer-derived writer and byte-match gate.
writers: circuit-next engine (reducer-derived snapshot writer; authored by Slice 27c)
artifacts.trust_boundary: operator-local derived state; state.json is written only by the reducer-derived snapshot writer at src/runtime/snapshot-writer.ts (never by step executors). A byte-match against a fresh reducer pass over run.log is the re-entry gate. Slice 27c lands the writer; re-entry byte-match enforcement in resume lands in Slice 27d+ per plan.

artifacts.description: Byte-for-byte copy of the workflow manifest taken at run bootstrap, persisted at <circuit-next-run-root>/manifest.snapshot.json. Carries the exact persisted manifest bytes (base64-encoded for JSON transport) plus a SHA-256 hash over those raw bytes. The byte-match re-entry check rejects any manifest drift between bootstrap and resume. Slice 27c authors this artifact and its writer.
writers: circuit-next engine (manifest snapshot writer; authored by Slice 27c)
artifacts.trust_boundary: operator-local persisted state; written once at bootstrap by the manifest snapshot writer at src/runtime/manifest-snapshot-writer.ts. Hash algorithm: SHA-256 over the exact persisted manifest snapshot bytes (`algorithm: 'sha256-raw'`), per ADR-0001 Addendum B §Phase 1.5 Close Criteria #8. Parse-time superRefine rejects any snapshot whose declared hash disagrees with sha256 over decoded bytes_base64; a second reader cannot be tricked into accepting a tampered byte-body under the declared hash.

artifacts.description: User-visible summary of what a run produced, persisted at <circuit-next-run-root>/artifacts/result.json once the closing run.closed event is appended. Unlike state.json (reducer-derived, recomputable from run.log) a RunResult is written once at close and never mutated; it is the authoritative 'what happened' artifact independent of future log rewrites. Slice 27c declares the shape; Slice 27d wires the writer into dogfood-run-0.
writers: circuit-next engine (result writer; wired by Slice 27d dogfood-run-0)
artifacts.trust_boundary: operator-local persisted state; written by the engine at run.closed append. `outcome` must agree with the closing run.closed event's outcome and `run_id`/`workflow_id` must agree with the run.bootstrapped event (runtime-enforced at write time); the schema enforces shape only.

artifacts.description: Typed override value (model, effort, skills, rigor, invocation_options) contributed by one config layer. JSON-safe invocation_options; strict unknown-key rejection.
artifacts.trust_boundary: operator-local at user-global/project/invocation layers; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer; engine validates .strict() key closure and ghost-provenance rejection at parse time. Composition produces selection.resolution (separate artifact).

artifacts.description: Result of layering overrides under 7-tuple precedence. Identity-keyed applied chain; category-level precedence; ghost-provenance rejection.
backing_paths: in-memory at dispatch resolution time; <circuit-next-run-root>/events.ndjson persists the DispatchStartedEvent.resolved_selection projection
artifacts.trust_boundary: engine-computed; always reconstructable from ordered overrides

artifacts.description: Config-layer map of custom adapter name -> CustomAdapterDescriptor. Registry-key to descriptor-name parity is enforced; reserved names are disjoint.
artifacts.trust_boundary: operator-local at user-global/project/invocation layers; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer; engine validates reserved-name disjointness, own-property closure, and registry-key/descriptor-name parity at parse time.

artifacts.description: Pre-resolution pointer into the adapter registry or a built-in adapter. Three variants (built-in, named, inline custom). Not valid in the event log.
artifacts.trust_boundary: operator-local at user-global/project/invocation layers; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer; engine validates named-ref registry closure during resolution. MUST NOT appear in run.log (resolved-form-only per ADAPTER-I10).

artifacts.description: Post-resolution adapter: either a built-in enum variant or an inlined CustomAdapterDescriptor. Named references MUST NOT appear here. Shape written to DispatchStartedEvent.adapter.
artifacts.trust_boundary: engine-computed; authoritative for dispatch; replay-stable (named refs forbidden by ADAPTER-I10)

artifacts.description: Top-level Config record contributed by one config layer. Carries schema_version (literal 1), a DispatchConfig (sub-artifact adapter.registry), a record of per-circuit CircuitOverride (sub-artifact config.circuit-override), and a defaults object carrying a SelectionOverride. Surplus keys rejected transitively via .strict().
dangerous_sinks: surplus-key rejection at config root and nested defaults
artifacts.trust_boundary: operator-local at user-global/project/invocation layers; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer; engine validates .strict() key closure + schema_version literal at parse time. Composition of multiple layers is out of v0.1 scope (see specs/contracts/config.md Scope).

artifacts.description: Layer-identity wrapper around a Config. Carries ConfigLayer (default | user-global | project | invocation), an optional source_path, and a validated Config. Distinct from the merged effective configuration at dispatch time (which is not a LayeredConfig).
backing_paths: in-memory wrapper around config.root (no standalone on-disk form)
artifacts.trust_boundary: engine-computed wrapper around an already-validated config.root; each layer's inner config is re-validated through config.root before wrapping. source_path is advisory (v0.1 does not constrain filesystem validity).

artifacts.description: Per-workflow override slot stored in Config.circuits[workflow_id]. Currently carries an optional SelectionOverride; reserved for additional per-circuit override categories in v0.2. Strict-key rejection forbids smuggling new categories without a contract change.
dangerous_sinks: strict-key rejection (forbids `skills: string[]` v0.0 shortcut reintroduction)
artifacts.trust_boundary: operator-local at user-global/project/invocation layers; plugin-author-signed at the defaults layer. Record-key shape (WorkflowId) is validated by ids primitive; catalog-closure of the WorkflowId against installed workflows is NOT enforced at v0.1 (same posture as adapter.registry closure notes).

artifacts.description: circuit-next catalog entry for a plugin skill's L1 frontmatter. Represents the machine-readable descriptor a selection resolver can bind by SkillId and a catalog compiler can enumerate. Prose body (L2) and extra files (L3) are out of scope.
readers: circuit-next engine (selection resolver, skill set composition)
backing_paths: <plugin>/catalog/skills.json (compiled catalog projection, one SkillDescriptor per skill)
artifacts.trust_boundary: plugin-authored static descriptor; loaded read-only at catalog-compile time; never model-authored during a run

artifacts.description: circuit-next continuity handoff record. Carries goal, next, state, and debt narrative across session boundaries; identity derived from record_id which is used as a filename stem.
artifacts.trust_boundary: operator-local persisted state; may contain model-authored narrative and path-derived identity
artifacts.migration_policy: deferred; no transparent runtime parse of old Circuit records. If imported later, a separate migration-source contract is required.

artifacts.description: circuit-next continuity resolver/index artifact. Points at the pending record and the current attached run; determines which record is authoritative for resume. Singleton on disk — no in-body identity.
artifacts.trust_boundary: operator-local persisted state; determines which record is authoritative for resume
artifacts.migration_policy: deferred; no transparent runtime parse of old Circuit index. If imported later, a separate migration-source contract is required.

artifacts.description: Framing artifact produced by the Frame phase of an explore workflow run. Declares the subject, operator task statement, and success condition for the investigation.
dangerous_sinks: success-condition binding for synthesize + review phases
artifacts.trust_boundary: model-authored during Frame phase (orchestrator invokes the framing model/adapter); read-only thereafter within the run; not cross-run shared

artifacts.description: Analyze-phase artifact produced by an explore workflow run. Decomposes the subject (from explore.brief) into named aspects with evidence citations.
dangerous_sinks: synthesis input — aspects that don't round-trip cleanly will leak into the synthesis recommendation without trace
artifacts.trust_boundary: model-authored during Analyze phase (orchestrator invokes the analysis model/adapter); read-only thereafter within the run

artifacts.description: Synthesize-phase artifact — the explore workflow's primary output. A recommendation, decision candidate set, or investigation conclusion with explicit mapping back to the brief's success condition.
writers: explore synthesize-step (worker dispatch, role=implementer per ADR-0008)
dangerous_sinks: dispatch-result-to-artifact materialization (per ADR-0008 §Decision.3a) — the canonical synthesis.json value is produced by schema-parsing the adapter-authored writes.result payload, so an adversarial or malformed adapter response is the upstream risk surface that downstream readers inherit
artifacts.trust_boundary: model-authored during Synthesize phase via adapter dispatch (worker-dispatch step with role=implementer per ADR-0008); the role tag binds to DispatchConfig.roles.implementer at runtime, recorded in the five-event dispatch transcript with payload + receipt + result hashes per ADR-0007 CC#P2-2 + §Amendment (Slice 37); distinct-adapter / distinct-model enforcement is evidential at P2.4/P2.5 landing (weaker-evidence disclosure per ADR-0007 §6.4; Codex Slice 38 HIGH 1 fold-in — DispatchConfig.roles permits implementer and reviewer to bind to the same adapter at v0.2, and ResolvedAdapter carries no differs-from-prior-dispatch field); canonical artifact materializes from writes.result via ADR-0008 §Decision.3a rule; read-only thereafter within the run; the primary output artifact for explore workflows

artifacts.description: Review-phase artifact produced by an explore workflow run. Adversarial pass over explore.synthesis with verdict, objections, and missed-angle enumeration.
writers: explore review-step (worker dispatch, role=reviewer per ADR-0008)
dangerous_sinks: close-phase result composition — the review verdict is the primary signal the operator sees at close

dispatch-result-to-artifact materialization (per ADR-0008 §Decision.3a) — the canonical review-verdict.json value is produced by schema-parsing the adapter-authored writes.result payload; an adversarial reviewer-adapter could produce a verdict that over-accepts or over-rejects, and the close-phase aggregation inherits that risk
artifacts.trust_boundary: model-authored during Review phase via adapter dispatch (worker-dispatch step with role=reviewer per ADR-0008); the role tag binds to DispatchConfig.roles.reviewer at runtime, recorded in the five-event dispatch transcript with payload + receipt + result hashes per ADR-0007 CC#P2-2 + §Amendment (Slice 37); distinct-adapter / distinct-model separation from the implementer-role adapter is evidential at P2.4/P2.5 landing (weaker-evidence disclosure per ADR-0007 §6.4; Codex Slice 38 HIGH 1 fold-in — DispatchConfig.roles.reviewer and DispatchConfig.roles.implementer may bind to the same adapter at v0.2, so the Knight-Leveson boundary is a dispatch-machinery-routing precondition at v0.2 that becomes contract-guaranteed at P2.4/P2.5 config/parity enforcement); canonical verdict artifact materializes from writes.result via ADR-0008 §Decision.3a rule; read-only thereafter; adversarial by design

artifacts.description: Close-phase aggregate artifact — the explore workflow run's terminal result. Composes a summary + verdict snapshot + pointers to the four prior artifacts (brief, analysis, synthesis, review-verdict). The run's final return shape consumed by the run result consumer. Persisted at <run-root>/artifacts/explore-result.json — distinct from the engine-authored universal run.result at <run-root>/artifacts/result.json per Slice 39 HIGH 4 fold-in (option (b) path-split resolution). Per-workflow <kind>-result.json siblings is the canonical pattern for future workflow-specific aggregates (build-result.json, repair-result.json, etc.).
dangerous_sinks: run result consumer; operator-facing summary of the explore run
artifacts.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Close (close-step aggregates prior artifacts deterministically); terminal artifact — no in-run readers; cross-run reader is the run result consumer only; path-distinct from run.result so the engine's result-writer (src/runtime/result-writer.ts RESULT-I1 — single writer to result.json) and the orchestrator's close-step (workflow-semantic aggregate at explore-result.json) do not collide

artifacts.description: Close-phase primary artifact for the audit-only review workflow. Structured JSON successor to the reference review.md artifact; aggregates reviewer findings and carries the deterministic final verdict.
dangerous_sinks: close-phase aggregation from model-authored analyze dispatch output
artifacts.trust_boundary: engine-computed at Close by the default registered review synthesis writer from a model-authored analyze-phase dispatch result; tests/runner/review-runtime-wiring.test.ts proves schema-valid review.result materialization on the default CLI path. Read-only after write.
artifacts.migration_policy: Clean-break structured JSON successor to the reference Markdown artifact; no transparent parse of legacy review.md is attempted.
